frozen_components: Input constraint validators (LLM-generated, fixed per problem)
architecture.summary: Agentic Verifier is an LLM-based agent that iteratively generates discriminative test inputs for competitive programming code verification. The system interacts multi-turn with a code execution sandbox, executing candidate solution pairs on proposed inputs, observing execution feedback and behavioral outputs, and refining a hypothesis about distinguishing input generators. After multi-turn reasoning, it outputs a final input generator program. The verifier is trained via rejection fine-tuning on successful trajectories from a teacher model, then further optimized with GRPO reinforcement learning using sparse outcome-based rewards (validity + discriminativeness). At test time, pairs of candidate solutions are ranked by output agreement on verifier-generated test inputs.
trained_components: Input generator reasoning and action selection

biblio.title: Scaling Agentic Verifier for Competitive Coding

claims.statement: Ground-truth test inputs consistently outperform randomly generated inputs by a large margin on USACO, with random generation failing to close the performance gap even at scale

claims.dataset: five competitive programming benchmarks (USACO, LiveCodeBench, OJBench, ICPC-Eval, CodeForces)
claims.statement: Agentic Verifier achieves up to +10–15% absolute gains in Best@K accuracy compared to execution-based baselines
On Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking-2507 + USACO, Agentic Verifier achieves 73.1% Best@8 (+10.3 over Vanilla) and 74.5% Best@64 (+11.7 over Vanilla)
On Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 + CodeForces, Agentic Verifier achieves 49.9% Best@8 (+10.2 over Vanilla) and 50.9% Best@64 (+11.2 over Vanilla)
The 30B trained verifier outperforms the zero-shot 235B model by a large margin on Best@8 (82.4% vs 79.5% on LiveCodeBench, 47.3% vs 43.2% on OJBench)
On OJBench hard problems with Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507, Agentic Verifier achieves 44.0% Best@8 compared to 24.7% for Random Generator baseline, demonstrating +19.3 point advantage

Agentic Verifier exhibits stronger and more stable scaling trends as the number of test inputs increases compared to baseline methods, which show earlier saturation and diminishing marginal improvements

Benchmark test suites act as imperfect verifiers covering only a limited portion of the valid input space, leading to false positives; the agentic verifier can expose hidden behavioral discrepancies by discovering counterexamples
